prompt: Build the complete Forgewright platform from its 9 RISE specifications (rispecs 00-08). Forgewright is a graph-based agentic development platform that integrates PDE-to-Plan Pipeline, State Machine Creative Engine, Graph Relational Substrate, MCP Tool Surface, Visual Designer, Ceremonial Technology Runtime, Agentic Autonomous Development, Medicine Wheel Integration, and Platform Architecture. Technology: Next.js 15, React 19, Zustand, KuzuDB, Redis, smcraft, MCP SDK TypeScript, SVG components, Langfuse tracing. Use 30+ claude-opus-4.6 sub-agents as a software development team.

primary.target: complete Forgewright platform from 9 RISE specifications (rispecs 00-08)

secondary.target: PDE-to-Plan Pipeline (spec 01) — prompt decomposition → Four Directions → structured plan → SMDF seed → graph nodes
State Machine Creative Engine (spec 02) — STC = state machine, creative process as SMDF runtime with smcraft
Graph Relational Substrate (spec 03) — KuzuDB graph database with OCAP enforcement, Wilson scoring
Unified MCP Tool Surface (spec 04) — sm/ + pde/ + graph/ + ceremony/ + stc/ + session/ namespaces
secondary.dependency: State Machine Creative Engine, Graph Relational Substrate, Ceremonial Technology Runtime

secondary.target: Visual Designer (spec 05) — SVG canvas for state machines + relational graphs, MCP↔designer sync
Ceremonial Technology Runtime (spec 06) — Five phases as code, OCAP middleware
Agentic Autonomous Development (spec 07) — Spiral autonomy, directional permissions, checkpoint policy
secondary.dependency: Ceremonial Technology Runtime, State Machine Creative Engine

secondary.target: Medicine Wheel Integration (spec 08) — 7 npm packages as platform infrastructure
Platform Architecture (spec 00) — 5 layers, deployment topology, Next.js web shell
code from sibling repos (smcraft, medicine-wheel, mia-openclaw, coaia-narrative, mcp-pde)
30+ claude-opus-4.6 sub-agents as software development team with deep-search, validation, execution roles
end-to-end functionality — the platform must be fully functional, not stubs
each sub-agent goes through a cycle that makes them surely doing what they were meant to do
deep-search on reliable data, organize results within the repo around the rispecs
KuzuDB database instance, Redis cache, SQLite for QMD index

assumptions: Sibling repos (smcraft, medicine-wheel) contain importable code
Medicine-wheel 7 packages are published on npm or available locally
Redis is available or can be installed
The platform runs as a Next.js application with API routes
MCP SDK TypeScript is the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package
30+ sub-agents can run concurrently with claude-opus-4.6

east.text: Understand the full scope of 9 RISE specifications and their interdependencies
Map what exists in sibling repos vs what must be built from scratch
Design the 30+ sub-agent team structure and work decomposition
Identify the structural tension: scattered specs → unified platform

south.text: Deep-search smcraft repo for state machine runtime, SMDF format, codegen, MCP server code
Deep-search medicine-wheel repo for all 7 npm packages implementation
Deep-search mia-openclaw for agent runtime patterns
Research KuzuDB Node.js API, Cypher query support, OCAP patterns
Research MCP SDK TypeScript for unified server implementation
Study the RISE framework for SpecLang compliance in implementation

west.text: Validate each sub-agent cycle: deep-search → implement → test → review
Test end-to-end: PDE prompt → state machine → graph persistence → visual render
Validate OCAP enforcement: sacred knowledge never accessible outside ceremony
Verify ceremony phase transitions: agents cannot skip opening
Cross-reference rispecs: do implementations honor all integration points?
Validate MCP tool surface: all 6 namespaces functional with compound pipelines

north.text: Build Layer 1: Data substrate — KuzuDB schema, Redis session, SQLite QMD, filesystem stores
Build Layer 2: Domain engines — PDE pipeline, smcraft adapter, ceremony protocol, narrative engine
Build Layer 3: MCP Tool Surface — unified server with all 6 namespaces + compound pipelines
Build Layer 4: Visual Designer — SVG canvas, state machine view, relational graph view, MCP sync
Build Layer 5: Product Shell — Next.js web shell, three-pane UI, companion switcher
Wire Medicine Wheel 7 packages as platform infrastructure
Implement agentic development spiral with checkpoint policy

actionStack.text: Scaffold Next.js 15 project with TypeScript, package.json, tsconfig
Deep-search smcraft repo for SMDF format, runtime, codegen, MCP server
Deep-search medicine-wheel repo for all 7 packages
Deep-search mia-openclaw for agent runtime substrate
Research KuzuDB Node.js bindings and Cypher query API
Research MCP SDK TypeScript for server implementation patterns
Define shared TypeScript types (ontology-core types, OCAP, Directions, STC)
Implement KuzuDB graph schema — 10 node types, 11 edge types, OCAP metadata
Implement PDE-to-Plan pipeline — 4 stages (Decompose, Enrich, Assess, Plan)
Implement STC-State adapter — bridge STC charts to smcraft state machines
Implement Ceremonial Technology Runtime — 5 phases, OCAP middleware
actionStack.dependency: Implement KuzuDB graph schema, Define shared types

actionStack.text: Implement Ceremony-Protocol middleware for MCP server
Build MCP unified server — pde/ namespace
Build MCP unified server — sm/ namespace
Build MCP unified server — graph/ namespace
Build MCP unified server — ceremony/ namespace
Build MCP unified server — stc/ namespace
Build MCP unified server — session/ namespace
Build compound pipelines (prompt_to_machine, step_complete, session_open)
Build Canvas Engine — shared SVG rendering for state machines + graphs
Build State Machine View — SMDF hierarchical rendering, composite drill-down
Build Relational Graph View — Four Directions circular layout
actionStack.dependency: Build Canvas Engine, Implement KuzuDB graph schema

actionStack.text: Build MCP↔Designer WebSocket sync
actionStack.dependency: Build MCP unified server, Build Canvas Engine

actionStack.text: Build Next.js Product Shell — three-pane UI, companion switcher
Implement Spiral Tracker + Checkpoint Policy
Implement Oscillation Detector
Implement Multi-Agent Coordination (shared state machine, event pub/sub)
actionStack.dependency: Implement Spiral Tracker, Build MCP unified server

actionStack.text: Wire Medicine Wheel UI components into web shell
actionStack.dependency: Build Next.js Product Shell, Deep-search medicine-wheel

actionStack.text: Write integration tests — PDE → state machine → graph → ceremony flow
Write unit tests for each domain engine
Validate OCAP enforcement — sacred knowledge extraction impossible
actionStack.dependency: Implement Ceremonial Technology Runtime, Implement KuzuDB graph schema

actionStack.text: Cross-reference validation — all rispecs integration points honored
End-to-end smoke test — human intent → PDE → state machine → ceremony → graph → visual

ambiguities.text: Which medicine-wheel packages are published on npm vs need local linking?
ambiguities.suggestion: Check npm registry for @medicine-wheel/* packages; fall back to local workspace linking

ambiguities.text: KuzuDB Node.js bindings availability and version
ambiguities.suggestion: Check npm for kuzu package; verify Node.js N-API binding exists

ambiguities.text: Whether smcraft has actual implementation code or only specs
ambiguities.suggestion: Deep-search the smcraft repo for src/ directory with TypeScript implementation

ambiguities.text: Redis requirement — is it deployed or should we use in-memory fallback?
ambiguities.suggestion: Use ioredis with in-memory fallback via Map for development

ambiguities.text: Langfuse tracing — is this critical path or optional observability?
ambiguities.suggestion: Implement as optional: wrap with isTracingEnabled() checks

ambiguities.text: node-llama-cpp for local embeddings — is this required for MVP?
ambiguities.suggestion: Defer to optional; use stub embedding function for initial build

ambiguities.text: 30+ agents literal or target — some specs may merge into fewer agents
ambiguities.suggestion: Organize by natural work boundaries; aim for 30+ but prioritize quality over count
